availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

sourceDesc.p:
The following institutions have graciously allowed us to include images of letters and photographs in the Letters of 1916-1923 project:
American Irish Historical Society
Butte-Silver Bow Public Archives
Chicago History Museum
Cloyne Diocesan Archives
Conradh na Gaeilge
Cork City and County Archives
Cork Public Museum
Drogheda Museum, Millmount
Irish Capuchin Provincial Archives
Limerick Diocesan Archives
Local History & Archives Department, Kerry Library, Tralee
Longford County Library and Archives
Irish Distillers Pernod Ricard Archive
Irish Jesuit Archives
The Jackie Clarke Collection
Archives and Special Collections, James Hardiman Library, NUI Galway
Manchester Central Library
Maynooth University, Library
Medical Missionaries of Mary
Merseyside Record Office Liverpool
Military Archives of Ireland
New York Public Library, Manuscripts and Archives Division
National Archives of Ireland
National Library of Ireland
National Museum of Ireland
Public Record Office of Northern Ireland (PRONI)
Redemptorist Provincial Archive
Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew
Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland (RCSI) Heritage Collections
St.Patrick's College Maynooth, Archives
Trinity College Dublin, Manuscripts and Archives Research Library
University Archives of UCC
University College Cork Library Archives Service
University College Dublin, UCD Archives
Wexford County Archive

More than 2,000 transcribers participated in a crowdsourced transcription project to transcribe the letters. Currently, more than 3,000 letters have been transcribed, validated, and made available for publication.

body.p: Letters 1916-1923 is Ireland's first participatory digital humanities project. Begun in September 2013 as Letters 1916, it expanded its collection period to the end of the Civil War through a generous grant from the Irish Research Council.

Letters 1916-1923 is a window into what life was like in Ireland a century ago, told in the voices of those who lived through it. This digital collection includes letters held at institutions in Ireland and abroad alongside those in private collections. There are hundreds of letters connecting thousands of lives, commenting on topics from politics to romance, from public administration to medicine. Letters 1916-1923 adds a new perspective to the events of the period, a confidential and intimate glimpse into early 20th-century life in Ireland, as well as how Ireland was viewed abroad.
